stemHtml: Due to a wind-shear event during an approach to land, the headwind component suddenly increases just before the flare. If the pilot does NOT take appropriate action, this could lead to..

options.a: the aircraft "ballooning" and landing beyond the touch&down aiming point, increasing the possibility of a runway excursion.
options.b: a sudden drop in indicated airspeed and associated "ballooning" during the flare, leading to a heavy landing short of the touch&down aiming point.
options.c: the aircraft descending and its trajectory going below the intended descent profile.
options.d: the development of a loss-of-energy situation, requiring a swift increase in engine power.
